skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: A skill for writing internal communications including 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, and status reports using company-approved formats.
skill.summary: Helps teams write consistent, well-formatted internal communications that follow company standards and executive expectations.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 11 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections were pattern matching errors on normal text content. The 'system reconnaissance' findings were misidentified writing guidance. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings were markdown formatting backticks, not actual command execution. This skill contains only documentation and templates with no executable code, network calls, or security risks.

content.value_statement: Teams struggle to create consistent, well-formatted internal communications that meet leadership expectations. This skill provides ready-to-use templates and guidelines for 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, and status reports that follow company standards.
actual_capabilities: Generate 3P (Progress, Plans, Problems) updates for teams of any size
Draft company-wide newsletter summaries with appropriate sections and tone
Create FAQ responses based on common employee questions
Write general internal communications for various audiences and purposes
Format communications according to company style guidelines
Adapt tone and content for different stakeholder groups
limitations: Requires access to company tools (Slack, email, documents) to gather real-time information
Cannot verify accuracy of company information without authoritative sources
Templates follow specific formats that may not match all company cultures
Does not replace executive approval for sensitive announcements

use_cases.title: Weekly Team 3P Updates
use_cases.description: Team leads can quickly generate weekly Progress, Plans, Problems updates for executive review. The skill formats team accomplishments, upcoming priorities, and blockers into the standardized 3P format that leadership expects.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers and team leads reporting to executives

use_cases.title: Company Newsletter Creation
use_cases.description: HR or communications teams can produce engaging company-wide newsletters summarizing recent achievements, announcements, and updates across all departments in an accessible, scannable format.
use_cases.target_user: Internal communications or HR teams managing company-wide updates

use_cases.title: FAQ Documentation
use_cases.description: During periods of change or uncertainty, the skill helps identify and answer common employee questions by synthesizing information from company communications into clear, authoritative responses.
use_cases.target_user: People operations or executive teams addressing employee questions

prompt_templates.title: Basic 3P Update Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3P update for my [team name] covering the week of [dates]. Here are our accomplishments: [list]. Our priorities for next week: [list]. Current blockers: [list].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick weekly update with all information provided

prompt_templates.title: Newsletter from Raw Content
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to write this week's company newsletter. Here are the key events and announcements: [paste links, events, achievements]. Organize this into sections and format it for company-wide distribution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Transform raw information into a polished newsletter

prompt_templates.title: FAQ from Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Employees have been asking these questions: [list questions from Slack, email, or meetings]. Create FAQ responses with clear answers and link to authoritative sources where possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Consolidate scattered questions into official FAQ format

prompt_templates.title: General Communication with Context
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to communicate [topic] to [audience]. The purpose is to [inform/request action/announce change]. The tone should be [formal/casual/urgent]. Key points to include: [list]. Please draft this communication.
prompt_templates.scenario: Custom communication for specific audience and purpose

output_examples.input: Create a 3P update for the Platform Team covering February 17-24. Progress: Launched new API gateway, reduced latency 40%. Plans: Begin database migration next week. Problems: Need 2 more backend engineers.
output: Progress: Launched new API gateway with 40% latency reduction. Completed Q1 infrastructure review.
Plans: Begin database migration sprint. Evaluate caching solutions for high-traffic endpoints.
Problems: Team at capacity - need 2 backend engineers to maintain velocity on Q2 roadmap.

output_examples.input: Write an FAQ response for: 'When is the next all-hands meeting?'
output: Question: When is the next all-hands meeting?

Answer: The next company all-hands is scheduled for [date] at [time] in [location/virtual link]. Calendar invitations have been sent to all employees. Submit questions in advance via the all-hands Slack channel.

best_practices: Gather information from authoritative sources (Slack announcements, executive emails, official documents) before drafting communications
Match the communication format and tone to the intended audience - executives prefer concise data-driven updates, while company-wide communications should be engaging and accessible
Always include links to source materials and related documents so readers can access additional context
anti_patterns: Writing communications without confirming the team name, time period, or audience - always clarify these basics first
Including overly granular details in executive communications - keep 3P updates to 1-3 sentences per section
Publishing communications without verifying facts against official company sources - accuracy matters more than speed

faq.question: What types of internal communications can this skill help me write?
faq.answer: This skill supports four main formats: 3P updates (Progress, Plans, Problems) for executive reporting, company newsletters for company-wide distribution, FAQ responses for common employee questions, and general communications for other purposes.

faq.question: Do I need to give Claude access to my company tools?
faq.answer: For best results, yes. The skill can pull information from Slack, email, calendars, and documents to gather relevant content. Without tool access, you can provide the information directly and the skill will format it appropriately.

faq.question: What is the appropriate length for a 3P update?
faq.answer: 3P updates should be concise - each section (Progress, Plans, Problems) should be 1-3 sentences maximum. The entire update should take 30-60 seconds to read. Focus on shipped work, priorities, and blockers with metrics where possible.

faq.question: Can this skill write communications for sensitive topics?
faq.answer: The skill can help draft communications on any topic, but sensitive announcements (layoffs, leadership changes, security incidents) should always be reviewed by legal and executive leadership before distribution.

faq.question: How do I choose between the different communication formats?
faq.answer: Use 3P updates for weekly team reporting to leadership. Use company newsletters for company-wide summaries. Use FAQ format when addressing common questions. Use general communications for announcements, memos, or other purposes that don't fit standard formats.

faq.question: What tone should internal communications use?
faq.answer: Tone depends on audience and purpose. Executive communications should be matter-of-fact and data-driven. Company-wide communications can be more engaging and use emojis. Always use active voice and put important information first.
